risk_if_not_implemented: Without Ownership Change Monitoring, security events may go undetected, allowing threats to persist unnoticed and increasing breach dwell time.

compensating_control_1.name: Third-Party Management
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the implementation of third-party management controls.
compensating_control_1.justification: Third-Party Management (TPM-01) provides third-party oversight that compensates for the absence of Ownership Change Monitoring (TPM-12.1) by extending security obligations contractually and monitoring third-party risk in lieu of direct control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Statutory, Regulatory & Contractual Compliance
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the identification and implementation of relevant statutory, regulatory and contractual controls.
compensating_control_2.justification: Statutory, Regulatory & Contractual Compliance (CPL-01) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Ownership Change Monitoring (TPM-12.1) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
